What Good Looks Like: Movement segment-by-segment through upper back only, head supported, ribs stay down.
Common Mistake: Letting the lumbar spine extend or the neck fall backward instead of isolating the thoracic region.

Exercise: Standing lunges with hip IR + extension
What Good Looks Like: Pelvis tucked, glutes squeeze to hold extension while stance leg rotates inward.
Common Mistake: Arching the low back or letting the hip flex forward to fake internal rotation.

What Good Looks Like: Deep lunge with tall torso, elbow drives to instep, thoracic rotation opens clean to the ceiling.
Common Mistake: Rushing through positions or collapsing the back knee.

What Good Looks Like: Front shin square to body, both sit bones down, long spine as you hinge forward.
Common Mistake: Letting the front-leg hip lift off the ground — cheats the IR stretch.

What Good Looks Like: Back shin vertical against wall, glutes squeezed, ribs down, pelvis posteriorly tilted.
Common Mistake: Arching the lumbar spine to fake depth instead of locking the pelvis.

What Good Looks Like: Slow, controlled full-ROM circles, body locked and stable via bracing.
Common Mistake: Shifting the pelvis or low back instead of moving purely from the hip.

What Good Looks Like: Segmental flexion/extension through whole spine, pelvis and head lead motion.
Common Mistake: Rocking head/tail only without moving the intermediate segments.

Exercise: Quadruped banded hip IR / lateral band pull
What Good Looks Like: Quadruped position, band pulls laterally across working hip, rotate femur internally against the band.
Common Mistake: Moving the pelvis instead of the hip joint.

What Good Looks Like: Band pulls femoral head laterally while you drive into end-range IR.
Common Mistake: Letting the band slip too high on the thigh or not enough distraction tension.

What Good Looks Like: Both knees stay in contact with the ground as you pivot, pelvis leading rotation.
Common Mistake: Picking up the knees or hiking the hip to throw the leg across.

What Good Looks Like: Heels drive down, glutes fire first, knees press out against band at top.
Common Mistake: Overextending the low back or letting knees cave in (collapsing the band).

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Ramp up to a hard isometric push into the floor, then contract rotators to lift out.
Common Mistake: Skipping the progressive ramp or using explosive motion instead of slow isometric.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Heels stacked, pelvis perpendicular to floor, knee opens purely from glute contraction.
Common Mistake: Rolling the pelvis backward to get more range and letting the low back do the work.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Hips stay level, drive through heel of working leg, ribs down at top.
Common Mistake: Non-working hip dropping toward the floor or arching the low back to push higher.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Hinge forward on stance leg, rotate pelvis IR and ER while shoulders and back leg stay in one line.
Common Mistake: Losing the hinge and rotating from the upper body instead of the stance hip.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Straight line head-to-feet (also from top-down view), adductor does the lifting.
Common Mistake: Letting hips sag or using the top hand to push up instead of the adductor.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
Exercise: Dead bug — slow march with banded overhead pulldown
What Good Looks Like: Low back stays flat-pressed to floor the entire time, opposite arm/leg slow and controlled.
Common Mistake: Letting the lumbar spine arch off the floor when the limbs extend.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Gentle nod first (chin to Adam's apple), then a small lift keeping forehead parallel to floor.
Common Mistake: Jutting the chin forward or using SCM/big superficial muscles instead of deep flexors.

Section: Hip & Core A (Mon) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Ribs stacked over pelvis, arms fully extended, zero trunk rotation for the hold duration.
Common Mistake: Letting the band pull you into rotation or shrugging shoulders up.

What Good Looks Like: Back flat on wall, knees at 90°, knees stay behind toes, weight through heels.
Common Mistake: Knees drifting over toes, or sliding higher as you fatigue.

Exercise: Terminal knee extension (TKE) with band — right leg
What Good Looks Like: Quad contracts hard to drive the last 10-15° of knee extension against band resistance.
Common Mistake: Hyperextending/locking the knee backward or using the whole body to push against the band.

What Good Looks Like: Hips square, back leg long and straight, prop under front glute if needed to level pelvis.
Common Mistake: Collapsing onto the side of the bent-leg hip or letting the back leg bend/flop out.

What Good Looks Like: Loaded heel stays down, working-side toe can lift, chest tall as you sink into deep lateral squat.
Common Mistake: Rounding the back or letting the working-side knee collapse inward.

What Good Looks Like: Knee drives straight out to the side, hips square to floor, no trunk rotation.
Common Mistake: Rotating the whole torso to lift the leg higher or arching the lumbar spine.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Torso leans slightly forward, front shin stacks over midfoot, slow controlled eccentric down.
Common Mistake: Front knee caving in or staying too upright (loads the knee instead of the hip).

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Hinge at hip with long spine, hips stay square to floor, back leg extends as counterweight.
Common Mistake: Rotating the pelvis open or bending the stance knee like a squat instead of hinging.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
Exercise: Side plank with hip abduction (toe pointing to floor)
What Good Looks Like: Hips stacked and lifted, top toe points DOWN (IR), leg lifts from glute med not TFL.
Common Mistake: Pointing toes up/forward — shifts work to TFL and hip flexors instead of glute med.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Athletic quarter-squat stance, constant band tension, short controlled steps with level hips.
Common Mistake: Standing too tall or letting trailing leg snap back (loses glute tension).

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Opposite arm and leg extend to one straight line, hips and shoulders square to the floor.
Common Mistake: Arching the low back, rotating the pelvis, or shrugging the shoulder to raise the arm.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
Exercise: Dead bug — slow march with banded pulldown
What Good Looks Like: Same as Hip & Core A.
Common Mistake: Same as Hip & Core A.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Same as Hip & Core A.
Common Mistake: Same as Hip & Core A.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Controlled rotation driven from the trunk with ribs over pelvis, no hip sway.
Common Mistake: Letting the hips swing with the rotation instead of resisting through the core.

Section: Hip & Core B (Fri) — Main Circuit (3 rounds)
What Good Looks Like: Hips perfectly level as hand lifts, feet can widen for stability, core braced.
Common Mistake: Hips rocking side to side or dropping as you tap — that's a core failure.

Exercise: Wall sit + TKE right leg (see Hip & Core A)
What Good Looks Like: See Hip & Core A entries above.
Common Mistake: See Hip & Core A entries above.

What Good Looks Like: See Hip & Core A.
Common Mistake: See Hip & Core A.

What Good Looks Like: One leg extends straight out to the side, neutral spine, rock hips back toward the heel.
Common Mistake: Rounding the low back or letting the stretched leg's knee bend.

What Good Looks Like: Rotation comes from the thoracic spine, hips stay stacked over knees.
Common Mistake: Collapsing the hips/pelvis sideways instead of rotating through the upper back.

What Good Looks Like: Shoulder blades retracted/down, elbows at ~45°, dumbbells track over lower chest.
Common Mistake: Flaring elbows to 90° and pressing over face/shoulders (trashes the shoulder).

What Good Looks Like: Press in scapular plane, ribs down, full lockout overhead without arching low back.
Common Mistake: Hyperextending the low back and leaning back to complete the press.

What Good Looks Like: Full ROM chest to floor, straight line head-to-heels, elbows ~45° from torso.
Common Mistake: Sagging hips, flared elbows, or head poking forward.

What Good Looks Like: Slight forward lean, arms slightly bent, reach out to the walls with elbows higher than wrists.
Common Mistake: Shrugging traps up or swinging momentum to raise the weights.

What Good Looks Like: Elbows stay narrow and pointed up, full stretch overhead, lockout with tricep contraction.
Common Mistake: Elbows flaring out wide or cutting the ROM short at the stretch.

What Good Looks Like: Grip just outside shoulders, shoulder blades pull down first, chest rises to bar.
Common Mistake: Kipping/swinging, or starting with the shoulders shrugged up.

What Good Looks Like: Medium overhand grip (~1-1.5x shoulder width), drive elbows down-and-back, slight back lean.
Common Mistake: Pulling with arms only or going too wide (kills lat ROM).

What Good Looks Like: Flat back, dumbbell travels to hip (not armpit), elbow drives back led by the shoulder blade.
Common Mistake: Rotating the torso to heave the weight up (cheat reps from the trunk).

What Good Looks Like: Thumbs back/palms out, elbows at or below shoulder height, external rotation at the end.
Common Mistake: Pulling with elbows too high — turns it into an upright row for the traps.

What Good Looks Like: Elbow stays pinned at the side, full supination at the top, controlled eccentric, no swing.
Common Mistake: Swinging the torso or letting elbows drift forward.

What Good Looks Like: Thumbs up/palms out, lead with the backs of the hands, squeeze shoulder blades together.
Common Mistake: Gripping overhand and cranking with traps instead of rear delts.
